docs.title: API documentation
docs.hint: Embedded Swagger UI from the API server. If the frame is empty, check NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and CORS.

history.title: Recent threads
history.empty: Open a thread from the queue to add it here.

settings.title: Settings
settings.intro: Preferences are stored in this browser only.
settings.soundNewThread: Sound when new threads appear (tab in background)

supervisor.title: Supervisor
supervisor.intro: Audit log and future reporting require INTERNAL_ADMIN_TOKEN on the Next server.
supervisor.reportsBody: Operator stats and queue charts will appear here after reporting APIs are added.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.body: CRM, translation, and ticket creation can be wired here when product priorities are set. Service Worker and offline drafts are tracked as optional follow-ups.

home.title: Operator console
home.intro: Operator replies use POST /internal/admin/v1/sessions/…/threads/…/reply with Bearer INTERNAL_ADMIN_TOKEN on the API (when set). See docs/FRONTEND_TZ.md.
home.threadListCardDesc: Open the inbox scrolled to threads
home.apiDocCardDesc: OpenAPI / Swagger on the API server

inbox.title: Chat queue
inbox.intro: Enter session_id and thread_id (from chat API headers or body). Token is INTERNAL_ADMIN_TOKEN on the API; leave empty in dev if the API has no token set.
inbox.securityHint: In production, avoid putting a long-lived admin token in the browser — XSS could leak it. Prefer BFF (the button below uses a server-side token).
inbox.bffHint: On the Next server set INTERNAL_ADMIN_TOKEN and optionally SPEKTORS_API_URL (otherwise NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL is used).
inbox.bffUnavailable: BFF unavailable: INTERNAL_ADMIN_TOKEN is not set on the Next server. Use direct mode below or add variables to .env.local.
inbox.successHint: The client will see the reply after the chat syncs.
